context7.description: Live documentation lookup — use with /docs command and documentation-lookup skill (resolve-library-id, query-docs).

exa.description: Web search, research, and data ingestion via Exa API — prefer task-scoped use for broader research after GitHub search and primary docs

figma.description: Figma design access — read designs, get screenshots, inspect components, manage Code Connect mappings. Use when the user shares a figma.com URL or asks about a Figma design.

unityMCP.description: Unity Editor bridge (localhost or remote) — query scene hierarchy, inspect GameObjects, read component values, run editor scripts. Requires Unity project open with the MCP server running.

_comments.usage: If you are using this mcp as claude code plugin, copy the servers you need to your ~/.claude.json mcpServers section
_comments.env_vars: Replace GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN placeholders with actual values
_comments.disabling: Use disabledMcpServers array in project config to disable per-project, or simply remove it from this file
_comments.context_warning: Keep under 10 MCPs enabled to preserve context window
_comments.unityMCP: Requires a Unity MCP server package installed in the Unity project (e.g. com.unity.mcp or a third-party equivalent). You must configure the url yourself — use http://localhost:8080/mcp for a local server, or your remote endpoint if hosted externally. The Unity Editor and the MCP server must both be running before Claude can connect.
